verdict_reasoning: BAX is a pro-apoptotic BCL2 family member that resides in the cytosol at baseline and translocates to the mitochondrial outer membrane (endomembrane) upon apoptotic activation. It lacks any transmembrane domain anchoring it to the plasma membrane, possesses no signal peptide for secretion, has no GPI anchor, and has no documented plasma membrane pool or surface accessibility mechanism. While BAX does interact with mitochondrial membranes during apoptosis, this is intracellular trafficking to an endomembrane compartment, not surface accessibility from the extracellular face.
